subject: ELA
title: Advanced Grammar
description: Complex sentences, clauses, verb tense, and conventions.

questions.q: Identify the DEPENDENT clause: 'Although it was raining, we went to the park.'
questions.explanation: 'Although it was raining' cannot stand alone as a sentence — it is dependent.

questions.q: Which sentence is in PAST PERFECT tense?
questions.explanation: Past perfect = had + past participle (had finished).

questions.q: Change to PASSIVE VOICE: 'The chef cooked the meal.' The meal was ___ by the chef.
questions.explanation: Passive voice: The meal was cooked by the chef.

questions.q: Which sentence uses a RELATIVE CLAUSE?
options: The book that I borrowed was great.
questions.answer: The book that I borrowed was great.
questions.explanation: 'that I borrowed' is a relative clause that modifies 'the book'.

questions.q: Identify the SUBJECT in: 'Across the field ran the white horses.'
questions.explanation: The white horses are performing the action — they are the subject.

questions.q: Fill in with correct subject-verb agreement: 'The team ___ playing well.' (is / are)
questions.explanation: 'Team' is a collective noun treated as singular in American English = 'is playing.'

questions.q: What type of sentence is: 'Can you help me, or shall I ask someone else?'
questions.explanation: Two independent clauses joined by 'or' = compound sentence.
A semicolon joins two closely related independent clauses.

questions.q: What is the FUTURE PERFECT tense of 'finish'?
questions.explanation: Future perfect = will have + past participle (will have finished).

questions.q: Which sentence contains an APPOSITIVE phrase?
options: My sister, a talented artist, won the contest.
questions.answer: My sister, a talented artist, won the contest.
questions.explanation: 'a talented artist' is an appositive — it renames 'my sister' and is set off by commas.
